question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / Midnight.take / settlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker, given a maker-ratified Merkle root containing offers at multiple ticks near a fee-breakpoint boundary (e.g., TTM = 1 day - 1 second vs 1 day), selectively fill the offer whose tick/TTM combination causes settlementFee() piecewise-linear interpolation integer division to round down maximally, so that buyerAssets - sellerAssets underflows the true fee by 1 wei per fill, causing systematic claimableSettlementFee undercollection across many fills? Preconditions: market with non-zero settlementFeeCbp0/1, TTM near a breakpoint; Call sequence: maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) with root encoding offers at TTM boundary ticks → taker calls take() at TTM = breakpoint - 1 → repeat at scale; Invariant: claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] must equal sum of (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) across all takes; Scoped impact: systematic settlement fee leakage; Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity in [0, 1 day, 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, 180 days, 360 days] ± 1 second and assert settlementFee(id, ttm) * units / WAD == buyerAssets - sellerAssets for all unit amounts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / UtilsLib.mulDivDown / mulDivUp] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the asymmetric rounding in take() where buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = units.mulDivDown(sellerPrice, WAD) for a buy offer, causing buyerAssets - sellerAssets to differ from units.mulDivDown(_settlementFee, WAD) by up to 1 wei per fill, and by choosing units values that maximize this discrepancy across many fills, extract value from claimableSettlementFee? Preconditions: buy offer ratified via SetterRatifier with root containing offers at ticks where offerPrice mod WAD != 0; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() with units chosen to maximize rounding gap; Invariant: sum(buyerAssets - sellerAssets) == sum(units * _settlementFee / WAD) across all fills; Scoped impact: extractable value from fee rounding; Proof idea: differential fuzz units in [1, WAD-1] and compare mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) - mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD) vs mulDivDown(units, settlementFee, WAD).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / TickLib.tickToPrice / PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP] Can an unprivileged taker, using a SetterRatifier-ratified offer at a tick where tickToPrice() rounds to the nearest PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP (1e12), cause the effective spread (buyerPrice - sellerPrice) to deviate from the intended settlementFee by up to PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP due to the double rounding in divHalfDownUnchecked, creating extractable value for the taker at the maker's expense? Preconditions: offer tick chosen such that wExp() output causes divHalfDownUnchecked to round in taker's favor; Call sequence: maker ratifies root with tick at rounding boundary → taker fills → assert effective spread; Invariant: buyerPrice - sellerPrice == _settlementFee exactly; Scoped impact: per-fill value leakage up to 1e12 wei; Proof idea: enumerate all ticks 0..MAX_TICK and compute tickToPrice(t) - tickToPrice(t) - settlementFee discrepancy.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / HashLib.hashOffer / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged maker ratify a Merkle root containing two offers that are identical except for callbackData (which is hashed as keccak256(offer.callbackData) in hashOffer), where one offer has callbackData encoding a callback that sources funds at a favorable rate, allowing the taker to fill the economically equivalent offer while the consumed group budget is shared, effectively doubling the economic exposure of the group budget through the rounding difference in buyerAssets/sellerAssets between the two fills? Preconditions: two offers with same tick/maxAssets but different callbackData in same group; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified(root with both offers) → take offer1 → take offer2 → assert consumed[maker][group] <= maxAssets; Invariant: consumed[maker][group] must not exceed offer.maxAssets; Scoped impact: offer overfill via callbackData hash collision path; Proof idea: unit test with two offers differing only in callbackData, assert group budget enforced.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee / buyerPendingFeeIncrease] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified buy offer at TTM = 0 (post-maturity), cause buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * 0, WAD) = 0 while still receiving credit, effectively receiving credit with zero pending continuous fee obligation, creating a systematic underpayment of continuous fees for post-maturity credit creation? Preconditions: market post-maturity with non-zero continuousFee, buy offer ratified; Call sequence: warp to maturity + 1 → take buy offer (seller reduces debt, buyer gets credit) → assert pendingFee; Invariant: every credit increase must carry proportional pendingFee; Scoped impact: continuous fee leakage for post-maturity credit; Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity = 0 and assert buyerPendingFeeIncrease == 0 when continuousFee > 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / sellerPendingFeeDecrease / UtilsLib.mulDivUp] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified sell offer where sellerPos.credit > 0, cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease = sellerPos.pendingFee.mulDivUp(sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) to round up by 1 wei, reducing the seller's pendingFee by more than the proportional share, so that after many partial fills the seller's pendingFee reaches 0 while credit > 0, causing the seller to owe zero continuous fees on remaining credit? Preconditions: seller has credit with pendingFee, offer partially filled many times; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() partial fill × N → assert sellerPos.pendingFee / sellerPos.credit ratio; Invariant: pendingFee must remain proportional to credit across partial fills; Scoped impact: continuous fee leakage via rounding; Proof idea: fuzz sellerCreditDecrease in [1, sellerPos.credit] and assert pendingFee never goes negative or disproportionately low.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.updatePositionView / mulDivDown / fee accrual] Can an unprivileged lender, by timing their interaction with a SetterRatifier-ratified market to trigger _updatePosition at specific block.timestamps, exploit the fee accrual formula fee = postSlashPendingFee.mulDivDown(accrualEnd - lastAccrual, maturity - lastAccrual) rounding down to accumulate less fee than owed, and by repeatedly triggering updatePosition at 1-second intervals, cause the total accrued fee to be significantly less than the continuous fee rate implies over the full maturity period? Preconditions: market with max continuousFee, lender with large credit; Call sequence: take() to get credit → updatePosition() every second until maturity → assert sum(accruedFee) vs expected; Invariant: sum of accrued fees must equal pendingFee at maturity; Scoped impact: continuous fee leakage via frequent update rounding; Proof idea: invariant fuzz over update frequency and assert total accrued fee >= expected.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee / piecewise linear interpolation] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the settlementFee() formula (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) / (end - start) where integer division truncates, by choosing TTM values within a breakpoint interval where the numerator is not divisible by (end - start), causing the fee to be systematically 1 wei lower than the true linear interpolation, and by filling many small-unit offers at these TTM values, extract value from the protocol's fee collection? Preconditions: market with non-zero feeLower != feeUpper, TTM in interior of breakpoint interval; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() at TTM where (feeLower*(end-TTM) + feeUpper*(TTM-start)) % (end-start) != 0; Invariant: settlementFee must be >= floor of true linear interpolation; Scoped impact: systematic fee undercollection; Proof idea: fuzz TTM across all breakpoint intervals and assert fee >= expected floor.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / lif post-maturity / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF] Can an unprivileged liquidator, operating on a market where a SetterRatifier-ratified offer created debt, exploit the post-maturity lif formula lif = WAD + (_maxLif - WAD) * (block.timestamp - market.maturity) / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF (integer division) to obtain a lif that is 1 unit lower than the true linear ramp, causing seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, liquidatedCollatPrice) to be slightly less than the borrower's fair share, creating systematic under-seizure that leaves dust collateral? Preconditions: post-maturity market, (block.timestamp - maturity) not divisible by TIME_TO_MAX_LIF; Call sequence: warp to maturity + t where t % TIME_TO_MAX_LIF != 0 → liquidate with repaidUnits input → assert seizedAssets; Invariant: seizedAssets must equal repaidUnits * lif / WAD * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price; Scoped impact: liquidator receives less collateral than entitled; Proof idea: fuzz (block.timestamp - maturity) in [0, TIME_TO_MAX_LIF] and assert lif rounding.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / maxRepaid / mulDivUp overflow] Can an unprivileged liquidator, on a market created via SetterRatifier-ratified offers with LLTV near WAD and maxLif near WAD, trigger an overflow in the maxRepaid computation (_position.debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD * WAD, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) when lif * lltv approaches WAD * WAD, causing the denominator to underflow or the mulDivUp to overflow uint256, reverting the liquidation and freezing the unhealthy position? Preconditions: LLTV = 0.98e18, maxLif = maxLif(0.98e18, 0.5e18), debt slightly above maxDebt; Call sequence: take() to create near-threshold debt → price drops → liquidate() → assert no revert; Invariant: liquidation must not revert for any valid LLTV/maxLif combination when position is unhealthy; Scoped impact: liquidation freeze via arithmetic overflow; Proof idea: fuzz lltv in allowed set and lif = maxLif(lltv, cursor) and assert mulDivUp does not overflow.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / buyerAssets / sellerAssets / sell offer rounding] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified sell offer (offer.buy = false), exploit the rounding direction where buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD), causing the taker (buyer) to pay more than the maker (seller) receives, with the excess going to claimableSettlementFee, but the rounding-up of both amounts means the taker overpays by up to 2 wei per fill relative to the true price? Preconditions: sell offer with tick where offerPrice % WAD != 0; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() sell offer with units not divisible by WAD; Invariant: taker payment must equal sellerAssets + fee exactly; Scoped impact: taker overpays by rounding, fee overcollected; Proof idea: assert buyerAssets == sellerAssets + (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) and compare to units * settlementFee / WAD.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf / leafIndex] Can an unprivileged taker supply a leafIndex larger than 2^proof.length - 1 (bypassing the require(leafIndex >> proof.length == 0) check only if proof.length == 256) to cause isLeaf() to accept an arbitrary leaf as valid by manipulating the bit-traversal path, effectively bypassing the Merkle proof and ratifying any offer hash without a valid proof? Preconditions: proof.length < 256, leafIndex has bits set above proof.length; Call sequence: craft ratifierData with leafIndex = 2^proof.length → call take() → assert InvalidProof() reverts; Invariant: isLeaf must reject any leafIndex with bits set above proof.length; Scoped impact: ratifier bypass allowing unauthorized offer fills; Proof idea: unit test with leafIndex = 1 << proof.length and assert revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / continuousFee * timeToMaturity / overflow] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified buy offer in a market with MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE and very large TTM (near 100 years = 3.15e9 seconds), cause continuousFee * timeToMaturity to overflow uint256 in the buyerPendingFeeIncrease computation buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD), reverting the take() and freezing the market for large-TTM offers? Preconditions: continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE = uint32(0.01e18 / 365 days) ≈ 317, timeToMaturity = 100 * 365 days ≈ 3.15e9; Call sequence: create market with maturity = now + 100 years → setIsRootRatified → take() with large units; Invariant: take() must not revert due to arithmetic overflow in fee computation; Scoped impact: DoS of take() for long-maturity markets; Proof idea: fuzz continuousFee and timeToMaturity at their maximums and assert no overflow.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / claimableSettlementFee / fee direction for buy vs sell] Can an unprivileged taker, by alternating between filling SetterRatifier-ratified buy offers and sell offers at the same tick with the same units, cause the net claimableSettlementFee to differ from 2 * units * settlementFee / WAD due to asymmetric rounding (mulDivDown for buy, mulDivUp for sell), creating a systematic discrepancy that either over- or under-collects fees relative to the true fee rate? Preconditions: market with non-zero settlementFee, offers at same tick in both directions; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified(root with buy and sell offers at same tick) → take buy → take sell → assert claimableSettlementFee delta; Invariant: fee collected must be symmetric for buy and sell at same tick and units; Scoped impact: systematic fee accounting error; Proof idea: differential test buy vs sell at same tick/units and assert fee symmetry.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee / underflow] Can an unprivileged taker, using a SetterRatifier-ratified buy offer at a very low tick where offerPrice < settlementFee, cause sellerPrice = offerPrice - _settlementFee to underflow (Solidity 0.8 reverts), effectively making the offer unfillable and freezing the maker's ratified root for all offers at that tick? Preconditions: buy offer at tick where tickToPrice(tick) < settlementFee(id, TTM); Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() → assert revert; Invariant: take() must revert with underflow for offers where price < fee; Scoped impact: fund freeze / offer DoS for low-price buy offers when fee is high; Proof idea: fuzz tick in [0, MAX_TICK] and settlementFee at max, assert revert condition matches price < fee.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / maxAssets consumption / buyerAssets rounding] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified buy offer with maxAssets set, exploit the fact that consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets where buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) rounds down, allowing the taker to fill slightly more units than the maker intended (since each fill consumes slightly less than the true asset value), causing the total units filled to exceed maxAssets / buyerPrice? Preconditions: buy offer with maxAssets, buyerPrice not divisible into maxAssets evenly; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() with units = floor(maxAssets / buyerPrice) + 1 repeatedly; Invariant: total units filled must not exceed maxAssets / buyerPrice; Scoped impact: offer overfill by rounding; Proof idea: fuzz units and buyerPrice and assert consumed never exceeds maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / sellerAssets consumption / maxAssets for sell offer] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified sell offer with maxAssets set, exploit the fact that consumed[maker][group] += sellerAssets where sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) rounds up, causing the offer to be exhausted faster than the maker intended (fewer units filled than maxAssets / sellerPrice), effectively reducing the maker's capital efficiency? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / lossFactor / mulDivDown] Can an unprivileged actor trigger _updatePosition on a lender whose position was created via a SetterRatifier-ratified take, where postSlashCredit = credit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - lossFactor, type(uint128).max - lastLossFactor) rounds down by 1 wei, causing the lender to lose slightly more credit than the bad debt proportionally requires, and by triggering many small bad debt realizations, cause the lender's credit to be drained faster than the true loss rate? Preconditions: market with small bad debt realizations, lender with large credit; Call sequence: take() to create credit → liquidate() with small badDebt × N → updatePosition() → assert credit; Invariant: credit loss must equal badDebt * credit / totalUnits exactly; Scoped impact: lender credit over-slashed via rounding; Proof idea: fuzz badDebt in [1, totalUnits] and assert postSlashCredit >= expected floor.

Preconditions: market with multiple lenders, small repeated bad debt realizations; Call sequence: take() × N → price drops → liquidate() × N with small badDebt each → assert sum(lender credit loss) vs sum(badDebt); Invariant: sum of lender credit losses must equal sum of bad debt; Scoped impact: lender over-slashing via lossFactor rounding; Proof idea: invariant fuzz over N liquidations and assert totalUnits - sum(badDebt) == sum(remaining credit).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / withdraw / pendingFee mulDivUp] Can an unprivileged lender, after receiving credit via a SetterRatifier-ratified take, call withdraw() with units = 1 repeatedly, causing pendingFeeDecrease = pendingFee.mulDivUp(1, credit) to round up to 1 wei each time, draining pendingFee to 0 before credit reaches 0, so that the remaining credit has zero pendingFee and the lender owes no continuous fee on it? Preconditions: lender with credit and pendingFee, credit >> 1; Call sequence: take() to get credit → withdraw(1) × pendingFee times → assert pendingFee == 0 while credit > 0; Invariant: pendingFee must remain proportional to credit after each withdrawal; Scoped impact: continuous fee evasion via dust withdrawals; Proof idea: fuzz withdrawal sequence and assert pendingFee / credit ratio is preserved.

Preconditions: offer at tick = MAX_TICK / 2; Call sequence: setIsRootRatified → take() at tick = 2910 → assert tickToPrice(2910) == expected; Invariant: tickToPrice must be monotonically decreasing and within PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP of true value; Scoped impact: price rounding error up to 1e12 per unit; Proof idea: differential test tickToPrice against high-precision reference implementation.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / IMidnight.isAuthorized / coarse authorization] Can an unprivileged operator, authorized by a maker via setIsAuthorized(operator, true, maker), call SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) to ratify a Merkle root containing offers with ticks/TTMs that the maker did not intend, exploiting the coarse-grained authorization (not scoped by action or market) to ratify offers with fee/spread parameters that extract value from the maker? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlementFee / TTM = 0 post-maturity] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified buy offer post-maturity (TTM = 0), cause settlementFee(id, 0) to return settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP (the 0-day fee), and if this fee equals the offer price (offerPrice == settlementFee), cause sellerPrice = 0 and sellerAssets = 0, meaning the seller receives nothing while the buyer gets units of credit, creating a zero-cost credit acquisition? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / isHealthy / maxDebt mulDivDown double rounding] Can an unprivileged taker, filling a SetterRatifier-ratified sell offer that creates debt, cause isHealthy() to compute maxDebt = collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) with double rounding down, making maxDebt up to 2 wei lower than the true value, causing a position that is truly healthy to appear unhealthy and revert the take() with SellerIsLiquidatable()? Preconditions: seller position near health threshold, collateral and price chosen to maximize double rounding; Call sequence: supplyCollateral → setIsRootRatified → take() sell offer → assert SellerIsLiquidatable revert; Invariant: healthy positions must not be rejected by take(); Scoped impact: healthy seller cannot borrow due to rounding; Proof idea: fuzz collateral, price, lltv at health boundary and assert isHealthy() matches true value.
